title: Why does Donald Trump want Greenland? | FT #shorts

transcript.text: Here's why Donald Trump wants Greenland
in maps. On some two-dimensional maps,
Greenland looks huge, but on a globe,
it's nowhere near as large. It has just
57,000 inhabitants, and it's mostly
covered in ice. But look at where it is.
Right between the US and Russia. As the
polar ice melts, crucial new shipping
routes are opening up. That's one reason
why Trump says Greenland is so important
[music] for US national security. The
Arctic Island is also thought to have
significant offshore oil and gas and
critical elements the [music] US needs
like rare earths. Although some think
its potential reserves have been
overhyped. Trump critics say [music] his
Greenland fixation is about picking up
new real estate, a brazen expression of
US expansionism, hearkening back to
[music] when great powers carved up the
world. Trump has already attacked
Venezuela. After doing so, he made
hostile remarks targeting [music]
Colombia, Cuba, and Mexico, too.
Greenland is an autonomous territory of
Denmark, like the Pharaoh Islands. But
Trump has questioned Denmark's ability
to ensure its [music] security. He's
floated the idea of buying the
territory, and he hasn't ruled out using
military force. [music] Meanwhile, the
wife of Trump's homeland security
adviser posted this. [music] So, what
about NATO? The US is a member of the
Transatlantic Alliance, and so is
Denmark. So, a US land grab would cause
a rift. [music] Denmark's leader warned
it could spell the end of NATO. But hang
on, does America actually need to enex
[music] Greenland? The US already has
the territo's sole military base. Some
local [music] leaders say they'd welcome
more mining projects. Another idea being
floated is an arrangement where
Greenland [music] would gain formal
independence from Denmark, but hand the
US responsibility for its defense.
[music] Three Pacific nations have
compacts like this. Whatever happens
next, it's clear Trump [music] wants to
redraw the map.
>> [music]
